---
title: "IC_R001: Global Scripts Synchronization Rule"
subtitle: "Immediate commit, pull, and push for continuous synchronization"
chapter: "CH06"
category: "rule"
number: "IC_R001"
date-created: "2025-04-02"
date-modified: "2025-08-17"
author: "Claude"
type: "rule"
law: "Integration & Collaboration Law Implementation Rules"
article: "Article 1"
implements:
  - "CH00.MP014: Change Tracking Principle"
related_to:
  - "TD_P001: Deployment Patterns"
  - "SO_R006: Temporary File Handling"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article Overview {#overview}

**Integration & Collaboration Law Implementation Rules Article 1 (Global Scripts Synchronization Rule)**

This article implements the Change Tracking Principle by establishing specific requirements for synchronizing changes to the global_scripts repository. All changes must be committed, pulled, and pushed immediately after completion to ensure continuous synchronization across all environments.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Rule
Every change to global_scripts must follow immediate synchronization: commit → pull → push. No local changes should remain uncommitted, and no commits should remain unpushed except in documented emergency situations.
:::

# Section 1: Synchronization Protocol {#protocol}

## Subsection 1: Immediate Synchronization Requirement {#immediate}

When working with files in the global_scripts repository:

第一項：立即提交 (Immediate commit)

```bash
# After making changes to any file in global_scripts
git add [changed files]
git commit -m "Descriptive message about changes"
```

第二項：拉取後推送 (Pull before push)

```bash
# Always pull from remote before pushing
git pull

# Resolve any conflicts if they occur
# Then push changes immediately
git push
```

第三項：無延遲原則 (No delay principle)

- Changes must be committed within minutes of completion
- Pulls must precede every push to avoid conflicts
- Pushes must occur immediately after successful pulls
- No "batch commits" at end of day

## Subsection 2: Standard Workflow {#workflow}

第一項：完整工作流程 (Complete workflow)

```bash
# 1. Navigate to the global_scripts directory
cd /path/to/global_scripts

# 2. Check current status
git status

# 3. Review changes
git diff

# 4. Add changed files (be specific)
git add 02_db_utils/fn_dbConnect.R
git add 02_db_utils/README.md

# 5. Commit with descriptive message
git commit -m "[MP014] Update connection timeout handling

Increased default timeout from 30s to 60s for slower networks.
Addresses issue #42 from customer service team."

# 6. Pull latest changes from remote
git pull --rebase

# 7. Resolve conflicts if any
# If conflicts occur, resolve them, then:
git add [resolved files]
git rebase --continue

# 8. Push changes to remote
git push

# 9. Verify push succeeded
git log --oneline -1
git status
```

# Section 2: Commit Message Requirements {#messages}

## Subsection 1: Message Structure {#structure}

第一項：訊息格式 (Message format)

```
[Reference] Brief description (50 chars max)

Detailed explanation of what was changed and why.
Additional context or considerations if needed.

Relates to: [issue/ticket number if applicable]
```

第二項：參考標記 (Reference markers)

```bash
# Meta-Principle reference
git commit -m "[MP014] Implement change tracking for audit logs"

# Principle reference
git commit -m "[P008] Update deployment configuration"

# Rule reference
git commit -m "[R006] Clean up temporary files after processing"

# Multiple references
git commit -m "[MP014, R008] Enhance synchronization workflow"

# Issue reference
git commit -m "[#123] Fix database connection timeout issue"
```

## Subsection 2: Content Requirements {#content}

第一項：必要元素 (Required elements)

All commit messages must:

1. **Be Descriptive**: Clearly explain what was changed
2. **Reference Principles**: Reference relevant principles (MP/P/R) when applicable
3. **Explain Purpose**: Explain the purpose of the change
4. **Include Context**: Provide context for why the change was needed

第二項：良好範例 (Good examples)

```bash
# GOOD: Clear, descriptive, with context
git commit -m "[R092] Update universal DBI connection handler

- Added retry logic for transient connection failures
- Increased default timeout from 30s to 60s
- Added logging for connection attempts

This addresses connectivity issues reported by remote offices
where network latency is higher than headquarters."

# GOOD: Bug fix with clear description
git commit -m "[BUG] Fix null pointer in customer profile module

The customer_id was not being validated before use, causing
crashes when switching between customers rapidly.

Added validation and error handling.
Fixes issue #456."
```

第三項：不良範例 (Bad examples)

```bash
# BAD: Too vague
git commit -m "Update files"

# BAD: No context
git commit -m "Fix bug"

# BAD: No explanation
git commit -m "Changed timeout"

# BAD: Multiple unrelated changes
git commit -m "Various updates and fixes"
```

# Section 3: Conflict Resolution {#conflicts}

## Subsection 1: Conflict Prevention {#prevention}

第一項：預防策略 (Prevention strategies)

```bash
# Pull frequently to minimize conflicts
git pull --rebase

# Use feature branches for large changes
git checkout -b feature/major-update
# Work on feature
git checkout main
git pull
git merge feature/major-update

# Communicate with team about major changes
# Before starting major work:
echo "Working on database module refactoring" > WORKING_ON.md
git add WORKING_ON.md
git commit -m "Reserve: Database module refactoring in progress"
git push
```

## Subsection 2: Conflict Resolution Process {#resolution-process}

第一項：解決步驟 (Resolution steps)

```bash
# When conflict occurs during pull
git pull
# Auto-merging 02_db_utils/fn_dbConnect.R
# CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in 02_db_utils/fn_dbConnect.R

# 1. Review conflicting changes
git diff

# 2. Open conflicted file and review markers
# <<<<<<< HEAD
# Your changes
# =======
# Remote changes
# >>>>>>> origin/main

# 3. Resolve by editing file
# Keep best of both changes or combine as needed

# 4. Test the resolution
Rscript -e "source('02_db_utils/fn_dbConnect.R')"

# 5. Mark as resolved
git add 02_db_utils/fn_dbConnect.R

# 6. Complete the merge/rebase
git rebase --continue  # if rebasing
# or
git commit  # if merging

# 7. Push resolved changes
git push
```

第二項：解決原則 (Resolution principles)

1. **Review Carefully**: Examine both versions of conflicting changes
2. **Preserve Intent**: Ensure resolution preserves intent of both changes
3. **Test Thoroughly**: Test resolution to ensure functionality
4. **Document Resolution**: Include conflict resolution details in commit

```bash
# Document conflict resolution
git commit -m "[MERGE] Resolve conflict in database connector

Combined timeout changes from both branches:
- Kept increased timeout from feature branch (60s)
- Preserved new retry logic from main branch
- Merged logging improvements from both

Both features now work together correctly."
```

# Section 4: Exception Handling {#exceptions}

## Subsection 1: Permissible Exceptions {#permissible}

第一項：允許例外情況 (Allowed exceptions)

Exceptions to immediate synchronization are permitted only for:

1. **Network Unavailability**
   ```bash
   # Document network issue
   echo "$(date): Network unavailable, changes pending push" >> .git/SYNC_LOG
   
   # Push when network returns
   # Check for pending commits
   git log origin/main..HEAD
   ```

2. **Related Changes in Progress**
   ```bash
   # Use atomic commits for related changes
   git add module1.R
   git commit -m "[WIP] Part 1 of refactoring"
   
   git add module2.R  
   git commit -m "[WIP] Part 2 of refactoring"
   
   # Squash before pushing
   git rebase -i HEAD~2
   # Mark second commit as 'squash'
   
   git push
   ```

3. **Emergency Hotfixes**
   ```bash
   # Quick fix for production issue
   git add emergency_fix.R
   git commit -m "[HOTFIX] Emergency fix for production crash"
   
   # If can't push immediately due to ongoing incident
   echo "$(date): Hotfix applied locally, push pending" >> HOTFIX_LOG
   ```

## Subsection 2: Exception Documentation {#exception-docs}

第一項：記錄要求 (Documentation requirements)

When exception occurs:

```bash
# Create exception record
cat > .git/EXCEPTION_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).md << EOF
Exception Date: $(date)
Reason: Network outage / Related changes / Emergency
Expected Resolution: $(date -d "+2 hours")
Affected Files:
$(git status --short)
Uncommitted Changes:
$(git diff --stat)
EOF

# Notify team
mail -s "Git Sync Exception: $(hostname)" team@example.com < .git/EXCEPTION_*.md
```

第二項：例外恢復 (Exception recovery)

```bash
# After exception is resolved
# 1. Review all pending changes
git status
git log origin/main..HEAD

# 2. Commit with context
git commit -m "[DELAYED] Changes from network outage period

These changes were made during network outage from 2pm-4pm:
- Updated database connectors
- Fixed validation logic
- Added error handling

All changes tested locally before this delayed push."

# 3. Carefully pull and merge
git pull --rebase --verbose

# 4. Test thoroughly
./run_tests.sh

# 5. Push with verification
git push --verbose
git log --oneline -5
```

# Section 5: Implementation Tools {#tools}

## Subsection 1: Git Configuration {#git-config}

第一項：基本配置 (Basic configuration)

```bash
# Set up git identity
git config --global user.name "Your Name"
git config --global user.email "your.email@example.com"

# Set up pull strategy
git config --global pull.rebase true
git config --global pull.ff only

# Set up push safety
git config --global push.default current
git config --global push.followTags true

# Set up helpful aliases
git config --global alias.st 'status -sb'
git config --global alias.co checkout
git config --global alias.ci commit
git config --global alias.br branch
git config --global alias.last 'log -1 HEAD'
git config --global alias.sync '!git pull --rebase && git push'
git config --global alias.wip 'commit -am "WIP: Work in progress"'
```

## Subsection 2: Automation Tools {#automation}

第一項：Git Hooks 設置 (Git hooks setup)

```bash
# Create post-commit hook to remind about pushing
cat > .git/hooks/post-commit << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
echo "================================================"
echo "Remember to push your changes!"
echo "Run: git push"
echo "================================================"
EOF
chmod +x .git/hooks/post-commit

# Create pre-push hook to check for issues
cat > .git/hooks/pre-push << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
# Check for temporary files
if git diff --cached --name-only | grep -E '\.(tmp|temp|log)$'; then
  echo "Error: Attempting to push temporary files"
  exit 1
fi

# Check for large files
if git diff --cached --stat | grep -E '[0-9]+ MB'; then
  echo "Warning: Large files detected"
  read -p "Continue push? (y/n) " -n 1 -r
  echo
  if [[ ! $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
    exit 1
  fi
fi
EOF
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-push
```

第二項：監控腳本 (Monitoring scripts)

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Script: check_sync_status.sh
# Check if local is synchronized with remote

# Check for uncommitted changes
if [[ -n $(git status -s) ]]; then
  echo "WARNING: Uncommitted changes detected"
  git status -s
fi

# Check for unpushed commits
if [[ -n $(git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline) ]]; then
  echo "WARNING: Unpushed commits detected"
  git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline
fi

# Check how far behind remote
git fetch
if [[ -n $(git log HEAD..origin/main --oneline) ]]; then
  echo "WARNING: Local is behind remote"
  git log HEAD..origin/main --oneline
fi
```

# Section 6: Benefits and Best Practices {#benefits}

## Subsection 1: Benefits {#benefit-list}

1. **Minimal Conflicts**: Frequent synchronization reduces merge conflicts
2. **Current Codebase**: Team always works with most recent code
3. **Disaster Recovery**: Critical code backed up in multiple locations
4. **Transparency**: Changes visible to all team members immediately
5. **Reduced Integration Issues**: Smaller, frequent changes integrate smoothly
6. **Audit Trail**: Complete history of all changes maintained

## Subsection 2: Best Practices {#best-practices}

第一項：日常實踐 (Daily practices)

```bash
# Start of day routine
cd /path/to/global_scripts
git pull
git status

# Before making changes
git pull
git branch  # Ensure on correct branch

# After each logical change
git add -p  # Review changes carefully
git commit  # With descriptive message
git pull --rebase
git push

# End of day routine
git status  # Should show clean
git log --oneline -5  # Review day's commits
```

第二項：團隊協作 (Team collaboration)

- Communicate about major changes before starting
- Use feature branches for experimental work
- Review commit history regularly
- Set up notifications for repository changes
- Conduct periodic sync status reviews

# Implementation Checklist {#checklist}

:::{.callout-note}
## Synchronization Checklist

**Daily Workflow:**
- [ ] Pull before starting work
- [ ] Commit after each logical change
- [ ] Write descriptive commit messages
- [ ] Pull before pushing
- [ ] Push immediately after committing
- [ ] Verify clean status at day end

**Commit Messages:**
- [ ] Include principle/rule references
- [ ] Describe what changed
- [ ] Explain why it changed
- [ ] Add issue/ticket references
- [ ] Keep subject line under 50 chars

**Conflict Resolution:**
- [ ] Pull frequently to prevent
- [ ] Review both versions carefully
- [ ] Test after resolution
- [ ] Document resolution in commit

**Exception Handling:**
- [ ] Document exception reason
- [ ] Set resolution timeline
- [ ] Notify team if needed
- [ ] Recover promptly
- [ ] Include context in delayed commits

**Tools & Setup:**
- [ ] Git configured properly
- [ ] Aliases set up
- [ ] Hooks installed
- [ ] Monitoring scripts ready
:::

# Conclusion {#conclusion}

The Global Scripts Synchronization Rule ensures that the global_scripts repository maintains a current, consistent, and recoverable codebase at all times. By requiring immediate synchronization of changes, this rule reduces conflicts, improves collaboration, and ensures critical code is always backed up across multiple environments. This implementation of the Change Tracking Principle creates a robust foundation for team development and system reliability.

---

*Based on: Integration & Collaboration Law Implementation Rules Article 1*  
*Implements: [MP014 Change Tracking Principle](../../CH00_fundamental_principles/MP014_change_tracking.qmd)*  
*Related to: [TD_P001 Deployment Patterns](../../CH05_testing_deployment/principles/TD_P001_deployment_patterns.qmd), [SO_R006 Temporary File Handling](../../CH01_structure_organization/rules/SO_R006_temporary_file_handling.qmd)*
